author: ai
hypothesis: When orbit-launch velocity is held FIXED across cases, increasing imposed well_depth pulls the shell closer (smaller well_distance_min) and does NOT, by itself, drive induced spin. This isolates well depth from the orbit-velocity confound found in EXP-2026-07-23-002, where the auto orbit velocity was derived from well_depth (0.475 -> 0.619 -> 0.765) and therefore co-varied with torque.
controls_note: All three cases start from the identical shell state: x = well_radius*1.25 = 4.625, y = 0, vx = 0, vy = 0.6188 (the mid-depth auto value), theta = 0.35, omega = 0.08. Only well_depth changes. gravity is left at its default so the imposed-well restoring gradient still scales with depth via the target() potential; the launch kinematics no longer do.
falsifiers: well_distance_min does not decrease monotonically as well_depth increases at fixed orbit velocity
max_abs_spin still varies strongly (> 0.05 spread) with well_depth once orbit velocity is held fixed
